title: US Housing Basics Guide
summary: Learn about the rental process, key terms, and tips to avoid common pitfalls.

content.text: Renting in the US is quite different from China. Understanding the basics can help you avoid many common issues.

items.label: Campus Dorms
items.description: Most convenient, includes furniture and internet. But pricier and smaller space. Good for first year.

items.label: Off-campus Apartment
items.description: Flexible pricing, more space. You'll need to find roommates, sign a lease, and set up utilities.

items.label: Shared House
items.description: Best value, but requires more coordination. Find roommates through school forums or WeChat groups.

items.title: Set your budget and area
items.description: Consider distance to campus, bus routes, safety, and price.

items.title: Search online
items.description: Use Zillow, Apartments.com, school housing websites, or WeChat groups.

items.title: Visit in person
items.description: If you're already in the US, always visit before signing. Check facilities, neighborhood, and transportation.

items.title: Submit an application
items.description: Fill out the application form, provide ID and financial proof. International students may need a co-signer.

items.title: Sign the lease
items.description: Read the lease carefully. Pay attention to lease term, early termination fees, and deposit policies.

items.title: Move-in inspection
items.description: Document any existing damage when you move in. Take photos as evidence to avoid deposit disputes later.

content.text: Always read the lease carefully before signing! Pay special attention to: early termination penalties, deposit refund conditions, and maintenance responsibilities. If you need help with the English contract, ask friends or your school's international student office.
Many schools have dedicated off-campus housing websites and international student housing assistance programs. Start with school resources.
